title: Flight Status
heroImageAlt: Airport departure-board panel showing Colombo flights with on-time, boarding and delayed states
intro: Check whether your flight is on time before you head to the airport — and know what to do if it is delayed or cancelled.

sections.p:
Search by AIRLINE + FLIGHT NUMBER (the two-letter code plus the digits, e.g. UL 504) and date, or by ROUTE (origin/destination) and date. We surface the latest scheduled, estimated and actual times sourced directly from the airline.

For Colombo (CMB), you can also check the airport’s public displays at airport.lk. For most other airports the operator publishes live status on its website.

sections.h: Stay notified by email and SMS
sections.p: Add your mobile number and email at booking and we will alert you about gate changes, delays of more than 30 minutes, and cancellations for your flight. We recommend installing the operating airline’s app as well — push notifications are typically faster than email.

SHORT DELAY (under 2 hours): wait at the gate; updates are posted on the screens and announced.
LONGER DELAY (2 hours or more): the airline typically provides refreshments and, for delays of 4 hours or more, a meal voucher. For delays of more than 5 hours on most airlines, you are entitled to a free rebooking on the next available service or a refund.

Keep your boarding pass and receipts for any extra expenses — many airlines reimburse reasonable costs under their conditions of carriage.

The operating airline manages re-accommodation. You are typically entitled to ONE of:
• Rebooking on the next available service at no extra cost.
• Rerouting via another airport or with another airline.
• A FULL REFUND of the unused portion of the ticket.

Under some passenger-rights rules (notably EU 261, UK 261 and similar), additional cash compensation may also be due. We will help you understand which rules apply and start the claim.

If you booked a SINGLE TICKET that includes both flights, the airline is responsible for getting you to your final destination — it will rebook you on the next available service at no extra cost.

If you booked SEPARATE TICKETS, the second airline is not contractually obligated to help, even if the first flight ran late. We strongly recommend single tickets for connections, and at least 3 hours of connection time on international transits.

INTERNATIONAL departures from Sri Lanka: arrive 3 HOURS before scheduled departure. Check-in usually closes 60 to 90 minutes before departure, boarding starts 30 to 45 minutes before, and gates close 15 minutes before.

DOMESTIC departures: 90 minutes before departure is usually enough.

Factor in extra time on peak days (school holidays, festival periods) when CMB processes more than 30,000 passengers a day.
